events.title: Air Canada temporarily suspends some flights to New York and other locations
events.description: Separately, Spirit Airlines reportedly seeks emergency US government funds as Iran war keeps aviation fuel costs high Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Air Canada has announced a temporary suspension of flights from Toronto and Montreal to New York ’s John F Kennedy airport, citing rising fuel prices. The move comes amid growing concerns that airlines worldwide may scale back services as aviation fuel costs climb in the wake of the US and Israel’s ongoing war with Iran, which entered a fragile ceasefire earlier in April. Although Iran announced on Friday that the strait of Hormuz had reopened, helping ease oil prices, fuel costs remain significantly elevated after weeks of disruption. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Air Canada temporarily suspends some flights to New York and other locations

events.title: More than £1bn pledged for Sudan as humanitarian crisis deepens
events.description: Donors exceed funding target at Berlin conference but prospects for ceasefire remain distant More than £1bn (€1.15bn) has been pledged for war-ravaged Sudan at a conference in Berlin, eclipsing the funding target organisers had set to help mitigate the world’s largest humanitarian crisis. The financial commitments made on Wednesday will also help offset a chronic humanitarian funding shortfall in a country devastated by three years of conflict, where two-thirds of its population – 34m people – require assistance. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 More than £1bn pledged for Sudan as humanitarian crisis deepens

events.title: More than 38,000 women and girls killed in Gaza war, UN gender equality agency reports
events.description: The war in Gaza has inflicted a far higher toll on women and girls than in previous conflicts in the Palestinian enclave, with more than 38,000 killed by Israeli air bombardment and land military operations since Hamas-led terror attacks in Israel sparked the war in October 2023, UN Women said on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 More than 38,000 women and girls killed in Gaza war, UN gender equality agency reports

events.title: US launches fifth strike on alleged Pacific drug boat in a week, killing three
events.description: Wednesday’s strike brings the total of those killed in US military strikes on alleged drug boats to at least 177 Three people were killed in a US strike on another alleged drug-trafficking boat, the fifth such deadly attack in as many days, military officials have announced. US southern command said it conducted “a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations” in the eastern Pacific, without naming the alleged group, in an X post. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US launches fifth strike on alleged Pacific drug boat in a week, killing three

events.title: Sudan’s war leaves thousands of children alone and displaced
events.description: Sudan is entering its fourth year of civil war between the army and the Rapid Support Forces, a conflict the UN calls the world’s largest humanitarian crisis, with children among the main victims. In Darfur and beyond, thousands are displaced, many separated from their families, facing hunger, violence and deep trauma despite overwhelmed aid efforts.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan’s war leaves thousands of children alone and displaced

events.title: Pope vs Trump: Has the week of tension weakened the US president?
events.description: A week of escalating tensions has unfolded between Donald Trump and the Vatican, after Trump insulted the Pope on social media following remarks on peace in the Middle East. His behaviour shocked audiences worldwide and reignited debate over his mental health. The feud has further exposed divisions within the American right in a country where religion plays a central political role. FRANCE 24’s guest Scott Lucas, Professor of US and international politics at the University College of Dublin, examined whether this week of tensions has ultimately weakened the US president.

events.title: Deadly Russian strikes on Ukrainians ‘simply trying to live their lives’ must stop, says senior UN official
events.description: The latest wave of Russian attacks on Ukrainian civilians in Dnipro, Kyiv and Odesa, which killed and injured scores of civilians, was roundly condemned by Matthias Schmale, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Deadly Russian strikes on Ukrainians ‘simply trying to live their lives’ must stop, says senior UN official

events.title: NSW should think twice before banning ‘globalise the intifada’ after court struck down anti-protest law, legal expert says
events.description: Another lawyer says ruling ‘puts brakes on the Minns government’s ability to use executive power to minimise people’s rights to protest’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Minns government should think twice before imposing an outright ban on the phrase “globalise the intifada” in the wake of a landmark finding that could limit attempts to control speech and protests, a leading constitutional expert has said. New South Wales’ highest court ruled in favour of the Palestine Action Group and Blak Caucus on Thursday, striking down an anti-protest law introduced after the Bondi beach terror attack that gave police the power to restrict marches, including the anti-Herzog rally in February . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 NSW should think twice before banning ‘globalise the intifada’ after court struck down anti-protest law, legal expert says

events.title: Sudan war enters fourth year as the humanitarian crisis deepens
events.description: Sudan has entered its fourth year of devastating conflict, after fighting broke out in April 2023 between the Sudanese army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. The war has left millions displaced and, according to the UN, created the world’s largest humanitarian crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan war enters fourth year as the humanitarian crisis deepens

events.title: Displaced by Iran war: out of Lebanon, into Syrian crisis
events.description: More than 227,000 people have fled conflict in Lebanon for Syria. But there they have found a lack of housing, economic hardship and no real state services. Despite a new ceasefire, many say they will stay in Syria.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Displaced by Iran war: out of Lebanon, into Syrian crisis

events.title: World Court celebrates 80-year anniversary as the international legal framework is under strain
events.description: The United Nations secretary-general warns that international law is eroding
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World Court celebrates 80-year anniversary as the international legal framework is under strain

events.title: As the ‘world court’ turns 80, Guterres says law must prevail over force
events.description: The world is at a “moment of crisis” and countries must reaffirm commitment to international law amid rising violations and geopolitical tensions, UN Secretary-General António Guterres said at an event on Friday to commemorate the 80th anniversary of the International Court of Justice (ICJ).

events.title: Strength in numbers: Developing countries band together as Iran war batters global economy
events.description: Developing countries are banking on having a stronger voice in debt negotiations, following the launch of a new country-led borrowing initiative on Wednesday on the margins of the annual IMF-World Bank Spring Meetings.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Strength in numbers: Developing countries band together as Iran war batters global economy

events.title: No signs of regime collapse as Iran weathers weeks of war
events.description: After weeks of US and Israeli strikes, Iranians are watching to see if ceasefire talks could lead to negotiations, while uncertainty over the future of the conflict persists. Despite foreign calls for regime change, analysts say Iran’s leadership remains entrenched, as economic pressure, repression and war damage deepen tensions inside the country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 No signs of regime collapse as Iran weathers weeks of war

events.title: French soldier killed in attack on UN mission in southern Lebanon, officials say
events.description: A French soldier was killed and three others wounded ​while clearing a road in southern Lebanon in an attack that UNIFIL peacekeepers and French officials said on Saturday was likely carried out ​by ‌Iran-backed Hezbollah. In calls with Lebanese President ⁠Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, French President Emmanuel Macron condemned the "unacceptable attack", his ‌office said in a statement. Renée Davis, FRANCE 24's correspondent, has the latest from Beyrouth.
events.ticker_label: 💥 French soldier killed in attack on UN mission in southern Lebanon, officials say

events.title: UPS plane aborts landing in near miss at same airport where November crash killed 15
events.description: A UPS cargo jet aborted its landing at Louisville Muhammad Ali International Airport to avoid a smaller aircraft on the runway, reports say.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ UPS plane aborts landing in near miss at same airport where November crash killed 15

events.title: Germany news: Iran war not causing migration wave — Dobrindt
events.description: German Interior Minister Dobrindt says that no increased migration to Europe has so far occurred amid the Iran war. An explosion in an underpass in a western town has killed one person. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Germany news: Iran war not causing migration wave — Dobrindt

events.title: UN peacekeeper killed and three injured in southern Lebanon attack
events.description: A UN peacekeeper has been killed and three others injured after a UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) patrol came under fire in southern Lebanon, the mission said.

events.title: Middle East : Fragile US-Iran talks hinge on nuclear and Hormuz disputes
events.description: US-Iran negotiations show limited progress but remain tense, with disputes over enriched uranium and the Strait of Hormuz still unresolved. Donald Trump warns the conflict could resume if no deal is reached, while mediators say compromises may still be possible on nuclear restrictions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East : Fragile US-Iran talks hinge on nuclear and Hormuz disputes

events.title: Ben Roberts-Smith’s comrades say he ordered them to execute unarmed civilians, court documents show
events.description: Former SAS corporal allegedly placed man on his knees and ordered fellow soldier to shoot him, according to statement of facts Australian soldiers have told prosecutors they executed unarmed civilians at the orders of Ben Roberts-Smith or in complicity with him, according to a statement of facts tendered to the New South Wales local court. Roberts-Smith, a Victoria Cross recipient and once one of Australia’s most lionised soldiers, faces five charges of the war crime of murder, allegedly committed while he served in the Australian SAS in Afghanistan. Each victim was unarmed and present in a location where Roberts-Smith could reasonably have suspected insurgents to be located; Each offence was committed in a situation where there was no active engagements with enemy forces and the Australian Defence Force was in control of the environment; Evidence was planted or falsely associated with each deceased to enhance reporting that each of the killings was within the lawful rules of engagement; Each deceased was handcuffed, detained for a period, and questioned prior to their execution; None of the deceased was killed in a situation where the Australian Defence Force did not have effective control of the battlespace. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ben Roberts-Smith’s comrades say he ordered them to execute unarmed civilians, court documents show

events.title: Europe looks to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A coalition of nonbelligerent states led by France and the UK has said it is ready to lead an international mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. But that won't be deployed before the Iran war is over.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Europe looks to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz

events.title: World News in Brief: Myanmar amnesty, rising needs in Afghanistan, another power loss at Ukraine nuclear plant
events.description: Authorities in Myanmar released the country’s ousted president from prison on Friday, along with some 4,000 other people, as part of an amnesty to mark the traditional New Year festival.

events.title: South Koreans breathe sighs of relief as escaped wolf is returned to zoo safely
events.description: Nine-day search for two-year-old Neukgu gripped nation and sparked safety concerns for animal and public The internet in South Korea erupted in celebration as a two-year-old wolf that escaped from a zoo was captured safely after a nine-day search that had gripped the nation and made the animal a national celebrity. The male wolf, named Neukgu, burrowed out of his enclosure at the O-World zoo in Daejeon on 8 April. Animal rights activists questioned whether the wolf could survive outside the zoo and also worried he might be killed during capture, something that happened to a puma that escaped from the same zoo in 2018. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 South Koreans breathe sighs of relief as escaped wolf is returned to zoo safely

events.title: Macron says a French soldier was killed and 3 were wounded in attack on peacekeepers in Lebanon
events.description: French President Emmanuel Macron says a French soldier was killed and three others wounded in an attack on U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon

events.title: Nine dead, 13 wounded in second Turkish mass shooting in two days
events.description: A 14-year-old boy allegedly shot and killed nine people at a Turkish middle school, the second school shooting in Turkey in just two days.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Nine dead, 13 wounded in second Turkish mass shooting in two days

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 16 April: UN meets over China-Russia Hormuz crisis veto as humanitarian crisis continues in Lebanon
events.description: War in Lebanon continues amid the fragile US-Iran ceasefire amid hopes for direct talks between Israel and Beirut. Meanwhile, the UN General Assembly is debating the China-Russia Security Council veto on the crucial Strait of Hormuz crisis last month, with assembly president Annalena Baerbock saying debate must move “to action”, on stabilising the Middle East. Stay with us for live updates below and live Meetings Coverage on the veto meeting is here. App users can follow our live coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 16 April: UN meets over China-Russia Hormuz crisis veto as humanitarian crisis continues in Lebanon

events.title: Ukraine: Police shoot dead gunman who killed several in Kyiv
events.description: The gunman who shot dead "several" people and wounded others in the Ukrainian capital has been killed during an attempt to arrest him, authorities said.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Ukraine: Police shoot dead gunman who killed several in Kyiv

events.title: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages
events.description: The attacker, who took hostages inside a supermarket, was killed after a shoot-out with police, officials say.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages

events.title: French soldier serving with UNIFIL killed in Lebanon attack
events.description: The deadly incident comes just days after Israel and Lebanon announced a 10-day ceasefire.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 French soldier serving with UNIFIL killed in Lebanon attack

events.title: At least six killed in Kyiv shooting
events.description: At least six people have been killed after a gunman opened fire in Kyiv.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 At least six killed in Kyiv shooting

events.title: Israeli strike on Hezbollah more devastating than 2024 pager attack, IDF says
events.description: Israel says roughly 50 aircraft struck more than 100 Hezbollah targets across Lebanon in a devastating 60-second operation that killed 250 operatives.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Israeli strike on Hezbollah more devastating than 2024 pager attack, IDF says

events.title: US Live Nation and Ticketmaster verdict triggers calls for Australian investigation into ticketing rules
events.description: One insider estimates Australians pay A$10 in fees per ticket, with fans bearing the burden of monopolised music tour schedules and inflated artist values Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia is being urged to improve ticketing transparency after a US federal court found Live Nation Entertainment had a harmful monopoly over big concert venues. This week, a New York jury found the global entertainment giant and its subsidiary Ticketmaster liable for systematically stifling competition to extract excessive profits from concertgoers. The jury identified a baseline overcharge of US$1.72 for every ticket sold by Live Nation since 2010 – totalling an additional US$595m in 2025 alone. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 US Live Nation and Ticketmaster verdict triggers calls for Australian investigation into ticketing rules

events.title: Shockwaves of Middle East war reach Caribbean as food prices soar
events.description: Six weeks since war erupted in the Middle East, the shockwaves have spread to the Caribbean region, already pushed to the brink, amid fears of a looming El Niño-linked climate disaster.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Shockwaves of Middle East war reach Caribbean as food prices soar

events.title: 2 US Army soldiers in Alaska injured in bear attack during training exercise
events.description: Two Army soldiers were attacked by a brown bear in Alaska during a training exercise at at Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson's Arctic Valley training area.

events.title: Gender, geography and powerbroking in play in race for next UN chief
events.description: The choice of the tenth UN Secretary-General, who will take office in January 2027, could shape global diplomacy, the response to crises across the world and the direction of the multilateral system for the next decade.

events.title: Jacinta Allan wants voters to see Victoria’s 12-year-old Labor government as ‘new and united’. Can she cling to power?
events.description: With a reshuffled cabinet, the premier is hoping to quell leadership rumblings as her party seeks an unprecedented fourth term Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast As the Victorian premier, Jacinta Allan, stood alongside the fresh faces in her reshuffled cabinet on Wednesday, she attempted to send her increasingly jaded electorate a blunt message: despite its 12 years in power, her government is – apparently – new. In her opening four-minute preamble to reporters, Allan - whose Labor government will in November seek an unprecedented fourth term - repeated the word 17 times. In one sentence alone, she referred to her “new cabinet”, “new portfolios”, “new solutions” and “new areas that are going to drive this government forward”. Benita Kolovos is Guardian Australia’s Victorian state correspondent Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Jacinta Allan wants voters to see Victoria’s 12-year-old Labor government as ‘new and united’. Can she cling to power?

events.title: Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis
events.description: A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis

events.title: Record fuel prices: How are governments responding?
events.description: In Germany, a liter of diesel costs up to 40% more than before the Iran war and other countries have seen even bigger price jumps. What are governments around the world doing to combat record fuel prices?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Record fuel prices: How are governments responding?

events.title: Italy's Meloni breaks with Trump over war in Iran, pope
events.description: The war in Iran and a spat between two of the world's most influential Americans has forced Giorgia Meloni to choose sides, potentially pushing the Italian prime minister to align herself with Europe's center-right.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Italy's Meloni breaks with Trump over war in Iran, pope

events.title: Pope Leo XIV condemns 'logic of extractivism' in Angola visit
events.description: Pope Leo XIV denounced the “social and environmental disasters” linked to a “logic of extractivism” on Saturday, the first day of his visit to Angola, a country marked by decades of exploitation of its vast resources.

events.title: At least five dead in Kyiv shooting, suspected gunman killed
events.description: At least five people were killed in a shooting Saturday in a residential neighborhood of Kyiv, and 10 were hospitalized, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced. The suspected gunman was shot during an arrest attempt, officials said.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 At least five dead in Kyiv shooting, suspected gunman killed

events.title: Why lower fertility does not have to mean economic decline
events.description: Globally, most people say they want two or more children, but many are having only one, or none at all. According to a senior UN economist, fears of a demographic timebomb are unwarranted.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why lower fertility does not have to mean economic decline

events.title: Iran to execute first female protester tied to anti-regime unrest
events.description: Iran prepares to execute its first female protester tied to the 2026 Tehran uprising as human rights groups warn of a deepening crackdown.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran to execute first female protester tied to anti-regime unrest

events.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
events.description: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

events.title: Sudanese refugees trapped between borders and bureaucracy in Morocco
events.description: A growing number of Sudanese refugees are arriving into Morocco after fleeing war, but find themselves stuck.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudanese refugees trapped between borders and bureaucracy in Morocco

events.title: Ukraine police shoot dead gunman who killed six in Kyiv, took hostages
events.description: The attacker has been identified as a "58-year-old Moscow man", but no motive has been established.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Ukraine police shoot dead gunman who killed six in Kyiv, took hostages

events.title: Sinlaku rips through Northern Mariana Islands as strongest tropical cyclone this year
events.description: More than 1,000 people were in shelters across Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands as Sinlaku moved away Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Super Typhoon Sinlaku hammered the Northern Mariana Islands, flipping over cars, toppling utility poles and ripping away tin roofs. Authorities were just beginning to assess the damage left behind by the typhoon, which first hit the islands on Tuesday night local time and continued with a barrage of fierce winds and relentless rains for hours on Wednesday. So far, there have been no reports of deaths. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Sinlaku rips through Northern Mariana Islands as strongest tropical cyclone this year

events.title: Ben Roberts-Smith released from prison on bail after being charged with five counts of war crime murder
events.description: Former SAS corporal granted bail ahead of potential trial on charges relating to alleged killing of civilians in Afghanistan Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Ben Roberts-Smith has been granted bail under strict conditions while he awaits a potential trial on alleged war crimes. The Victoria Cross recipient, once Australia’s most lionised soldier, faces five charges of war crime murder over allegations he killed unarmed civilians during his service with the Australian SAS in Afghanistan. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ben Roberts-Smith released from prison on bail after being charged with five counts of war crime murder

events.title: British police probe another arson attack in London
events.description: The UK has seen a spate of similar incidents in recent weeks, heightening concerns over growing antisemitism in the country.

events.title: UNIFIL sheds light on southern Lebanon attack that killed French soldier
events.description: A French soldier was killed in southern Lebanon on Saturday 18, while serving with UNIFIL, the United Nations peacekeeping force. UNIFIL deputy spokesperson Tilak Pokharel was FRANCE 24’s guest this afternoon to explain what happened and what is currently known about those responsible.

events.title: Turkey’s economy hit as Syrians return home
events.description: Since the end of the war in Syria last year, many Syrians have left Turkey to return home. Of the 3.6 million refugees living in the country, more than half a million are believed to have already gone back. These departures are weakening the Turkish economy, which relies heavily on low-cost labour, particularly in the textile and agricultural sectors. In the south-east, in Gaziantep, half of the garment workshops closed last year due to a shortage of workers. A report by Jenna Le Bras and Adrià Rocha Cutiller.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Turkey’s economy hit as Syrians return home

events.title: Families in Iran struggle with school closures even as the airstrikes have stopped
events.description: Schools have been closed across Iran since the United States and Israel launched the war on Feb. 28, with no word on when in-person instruction will resume
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Families in Iran struggle with school closures even as the airstrikes have stopped

events.title: Sudan: Three quarters of women feel unsafe as war rages on
events.description: Across war-torn Sudan, women and girls “are telling a consistent story of continued experience of danger, and risks for gender-based violence” whether when fleeing to safety or arriving at displacement camps, a senior official with the UN reproductive and sexual health agency UNFPA said on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan: Three quarters of women feel unsafe as war rages on

events.title: More than half of Haitians continue to face food crisis
events.description: 5.8 million Haitians, or roughly 52 per cent of the population, are facing crisis levels of food insecurity, or worse. Of those, more than 1.8 million are dealing with emergency levels, which means they are exhausting their last assets and unable to meet even basic food needs.

events.title: UNESCO grants ‘enhanced protection’ to cultural heritage sites in the Middle East
events.description: Since the Middle East war started on 28 February, several sites of major cultural significance have come under attack in Israel, Iran and Lebanon. Ensuring their protection is the task of the UN agency for education, science and culture, UNESCO.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UNESCO grants ‘enhanced protection’ to cultural heritage sites in the Middle East

events.title: 1 million bees swarm highway after crash shuts down interstate ramp for hours
events.description: A truck carrying 1 million bees crashed on Interstate 40 in Knoxville, Tennessee, closing part of the highway as beekeepers scrambled to contain them.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 1 million bees swarm highway after crash shuts down interstate ramp for hours

events.title: Australia's most decorated veteran walks free on bail on war crimes charges related to Afghan deaths
events.description: Australia's most decorated living veteran Ben Roberts-Smith was released on bail after being charged with war crime murder over killings in Afghanistan.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia's most decorated veteran walks free on bail on war crimes charges related to Afghan deaths

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Suspicion surrounds death of US influencer Ashly Robinson in Zanzibar
events.description: Lifestyle influencer died while on vacation with boyfriend, who local officials say has since had his passport ‘withheld’ Ashly Robinson, a US lifestyle influencer, died last week while on vacation in the Tanzanian islands of Zanzibar with her boyfriend, Joe McCann. Robinson’s death on 9 April, just days after her birthday and a marriage proposal from McCann, has sparked suspicion on social media, with users doubtful of the current narrative surrounding her death. No arrests have been made, and police previously said that McCann was not suspected of wrongdoing. But officials in Zanzibar released a statement on Tuesday saying that McCann’s passport has been “ withheld ”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Suspicion surrounds death of US influencer Ashly Robinson in Zanzibar

events.title: Sheinbaum vows to ‘defend Mexicans at every level’ amid anger at Trump over migrant deaths
events.description: Sheinbaum has recently been taking a firmer stance with the US, defying pressures where other countries have caved The Mexican government has voiced concern about the deaths of its citizens in US custody, with Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum also pushing back against the Trump administration’s decision to impose an energy blockade on Cuba. The progressive Mexican leader has walked a careful line with Trump for more than a year, addressing provocations with a measured tone and meeting US requests to crack down on cartels more so than her predecessors, in an effort to offset threats of tariffs and US military action against gangs. Continue reading...

events.title: Daniel Kinahan arrested over alleged links to 'international organised crime'
events.description: Irish national Daniel Kinahan has been arrested in Dubai over alleged links to an "international organised crime network".

events.title: Alleged Irish cartel boss arrested in covert operation on organized crime charges after years-long manhunt
events.description: International fugitive Daniel Kinahan, alleged leader of Ireland's Kinahan cartel, was arrested in Dubai this week on organized crime charges.

events.title: From Auschwitz, Holocaust survivor issues urgent warning over rising antisemitism in Canada
events.description: Holocaust survivor Nate Leipciger, 98, warns from Auschwitz about growing antisemitism in Canada, calling on the nation to stand up and take action now.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 From Auschwitz, Holocaust survivor issues urgent warning over rising antisemitism in Canada

events.title: What's it like to negotiate with Iran? We asked people who have done it
events.description: Experts who spent months negotiating a 2015 nuclear agreement with Iran say mutual mistrust, starkly different negotiating styles, and the complexity of the issues make a quick deal unlikely.

events.title: As Sri Lanka celebrated its New Year, the war in Iran was felt
events.description: Buddhists and Hindus in Sri Lanka marked their New Year on Tuesday. As residents in the capital Colombo celebrated, a war thousands of miles away was making itself felt.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As Sri Lanka celebrated its New Year, the war in Iran was felt

events.title: UNIFIL: Why are UN peacekeepers in Lebanon?
events.description: A French UN peacekeeper has been killed in Lebanon, highlighting rising risks for UNIFIL. The UN mission has been in the country for nearly 50 years — DW looks at its mandate, role and growing criticism.

events.title: New food alert platform helps humanitarians combat hunger
events.description: The UN World Food Programme (WFP) released its next-generation platform on Thursday known as HungerMap Live, a digital monitoring and intelligence site that integrates food security data with predictive modelling to help fight hunger in more than 50 countries.

events.title: Daughter of missing American in Bahamas says Brian Hooker using mother's illness as 'excuse' to leave country
events.description: The daughter of an American woman missing in the Bahamas accuses stepfather Brian Hooker of using his mother's illness as an excuse to leave the country.

events.title: As fentanyl crisis evolves, experts say US is still ‘behind the eight ball’
events.description: The Trump administration's militarised approach and cuts to health programmes threaten progress against fentanyl.

events.title: US strikes on drug boats spark legal controversy
events.description: The US has carried out further attacks on alleged drug boats. Critics say the strikes are not only ineffective, they are also largely about appealing to Trump's own domestic base.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US strikes on drug boats spark legal controversy

events.title: UN peacekeeping missions under strain as funding cuts and new threats grow
events.description: From election support in the Central African Republic to patrols in the disputed Abyei region, UN peacekeepers are operating in increasingly volatile environments – but shrinking resources and new threats, including drone warfare, are testing their ability to keep communities safe.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN peacekeeping missions under strain as funding cuts and new threats grow

events.title: Two boys dead after illegal immigrant from Mexico allegedly drove drunk and hit them on a sidewalk
events.description: ICE lodged a detainer for an illegal immigrant from Mexico who allegedly caused a deadly DUI crash that killed two boys riding bikes in South Carolina.

events.title: WATCH: Illegal street racing 'takeover' explodes as hundreds swarm streets and suspects bolt
events.description: Police say 18 people were arrested and firearms recovered after an illegal street racing takeover drew 200 to an Atlanta intersection Sunday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WATCH: Illegal street racing 'takeover' explodes as hundreds swarm streets and suspects bolt

events.title: Turkiye woos investors amid Iran war fallout in Gulf economies
events.description: Turkish officials are promoting Istanbul as a regional financial hub amid the war's fallout on the Gulf economies.

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Pedro Pascal v Pedro Piscal: actor in legal battle with Chilean spirit brand
events.description: Pedro Piscal pisco is latest Chilean brand to resemble a Hollywood name – and others have fought off the lawsuits The actor Pedro Pascal is waging a legal battle against a Chilean pisco merchant who has chosen a cheeky name for his brand of the country’s national spirit: Pedro Piscal. David Herrera registered the brand name with a Chilean commercial regulator in 2023 and began selling his pisco in off-licences and restaurants. Continue reading...

events.title: Caracas' iconic macaws threatened by vanishing palm trees
events.description: In the Venezuelan capital, Caracas, flocks of colorful macaws that once brightened city skies now face disappearing nest sites — and with them, a unique urban bond.

events.title: Nigeria students abducted as gunmen attack passenger bus in Benue state
events.description: Gunmen have abducted students on their way to university examinations in Nigeria's Benue state

events.title: Renowned physicist alarmed by 'unheard of' number of scientists dying or vanishing now on White House's radar

events.title: How South Korea plans to use the Iran crisis to spur a renewables revolution
events.description: Energy crisis unfolding in Middle East has added political urgency, and more funding, to transform South Korea’s solar industry In Guyang-ri, a farming village of 70 households about 90 minutes south-east of Seoul, people gather for communal free lunches six days a week. The meals are funded by the village’s one-megawatt solar installation, which generates roughly 10m won ($6,800) in net profit each month. “Residents eat lunch together every day, so we see each other’s faces, talk together,” says Jeon Joo-young, the village chief. “Bonds and solidarity between residents become much stronger. Life becomes more enjoyable.” Continue reading...

events.title: Weather tracker: hail covers parts of Tunisia and Algeria like snow
events.description: Accumulations of up to 3cm deep reported as severe thunderstorms also bring heavy downpours to central Italy Severe thunderstorms have affected the Mediterranean this week. On Monday, a surface low-pressure system in the Mediterranean in conjunction with an upper air cut-off low, led to thunderstorms over north Africa. Their intensity was aided by the hot precursor conditions. Algeria and Tunisia were notably affected by the thunderstorms, with some hail accumulation layers as a result. When so much hail forms, it starts to lay down sheets of hail, covering the ground like snow. Hail accumulations of up to 3cm were reported in Oum Ladjoul and Hammam Sokhna in Algeria, and there were hailstones of up to 3cm in diameter in Makthar, Tunisia. Thunderstorms continued in the region through the following day, with further hail accumulations, notably in Ouled Bousmir, Tunisia, where there was a layer about 2cm deep. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: hail covers parts of Tunisia and Algeria like snow

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 April: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins; Iran announces opening of Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon came into effect at midnight in Beirut, offering a pause in hostilities after weeks of fighting. The UN Secretary-General has welcomed the agreement and urged all parties to respect it, expressing hope it could open the way for further negotiations. He also welcomed Iran’s announcement that the Strait of Hormuz will remain open to commercial vessels for the remainder of the ceasefire, calling it a step in the right direction. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 April: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins; Iran announces opening of Strait of Hormuz

After 10 minutes, Irene gets off the bus, walks down an alley, and enters a damp, smoky shelter. Plastic tables fill the space and playing cards are scattered on the floor. events.title: ‘How do I end a call?’: the elderly Japanese people determined to master smartphones
events.description: Elderly people take advantage of courses on how to navigate mobile devices and avoid ‘analogue isolation’ It’s not only young people whose gaze is fixed on tiny screens. But for these users in Tokyo, clicking and scrolling is anything but second nature. “I can’t deal with all of the apps that jump out at me,” says one. “How do I know if I’ve definitely ended a call?” asks another. Continue reading...

events.title: M 5.7 - 20 km ESE of Silver Springs, Nevada
events.description: PAGER - GREEN ShakeMap - VII DYFI? - VII Time 2026-04-14 01:29:12 UTC 2026-04-14 01:29:12 UTC at epicenter Location 39.335°N 119.008°W Depth 5.00 km (3.11 mi)

events.title: Born in south Lebanon, displaced to Beirut, two grandmothers reflect on Israeli invasions
events.description: They grew up amid olive groves in southern Lebanon. The son of one married the other's daughter. Now they're living temporarily in a vacant building in central Beirut, displaced many times.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Born in south Lebanon, displaced to Beirut, two grandmothers reflect on Israeli invasions

events.title: India news: Modi slams Congress over women's quota vote
events.description: The Indian prime minister blamed the main opposition party for failing to pass a law reserving a third of parliamentary seats for women. The plan was tied to a redrawing of voting boundaries. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 💥 India news: Modi slams Congress over women's quota vote

events.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
events.description: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

events.title: Rutte dismisses speculation about US leaving NATO
events.description: NATO chief Mark Rutte said he understands Donald Trump's frustration with NATO. He also called on Europe to boost its defense industry.

events.title: US extends waiver allowing purchase of Russian oil
events.description: The US has renewed a waiver permitting countries to buy Russian oil for another month. The decision came two days after the Trump administration had said it had no plans to extend the waiver.

events.title: Teen discovers first ancient Greek artifact found in Berlin
events.description: A 13-year-old stumbled upon a coin from ancient Troy — an extremely rare find in Germany. Experts say it may offer new clues about little-known links between ancient Greece and northern Europe.

events.title: Kanye West concert canceled in Poland
events.description: The Polish government had urged organizers "not to make public space available to promoters of a criminal ideology." Kanye has disavowed and apologized his previous pro-Nazi stance, linking it to his bipolar disorder.

events.title: India’s harvest festivals under climate strain
events.description: As people across India celebrate traditional agrarian spring festivals, climate change has become an unwanted guest at the table. How are communities rising to the pressure on harvests, water, and rural life?

events.title: Europe's far right leaders gather in Milan rally against immigration
events.description: Several thousand people gathered in Milan on Saturday for a large rally attended by several European far-right leaders who spoke on immigration, security, and against EU regulations.

events.title: Replay : Donald Trump holds briefing amid Hormuz tension
events.description: Rewatch Donald Trump’s live briefing from the White House on Saturday, April 18, as Iran announced it was re-closing the Strait of Hormuz while the US maintains its port blockade. FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar has the analysis.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz : Iran to block transit until US lifts its blockade
events.description: Tensions continue to rise in the Middle East, with Iran briefly reopening the Strait of Hormuz before closing it again after around 20 hours. Tehran says the US is not respecting the terms of the agreement reached, casting further doubt on the fragile deal. Meanwhile, negotiations are still ongoing in Islamabad. FRANCE 24’s correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah, brings the latest developments.

events.title: Lens keep Ligue 1 title hopes alive with comeback win over Toulouse
events.description: Lens came back from 2-0 down to claim a crucial home win over Toulouse on Friday night. The title-chasers are now just one point adrift of PSG, who have two games in hand.

events.title: 'Very interesting documents' related to UFOs will be released 'soon', says Trump
events.description: Donald Trump has promised some "very interesting documents" related to UFOs will be released by the US defence department "very, very soon".

events.title: Fourth Kanye West concert cancelled
events.description: Another Kanye West concert has been cancelled amid a furore over his past antisemitic comments.

events.title: Cuba is on its knees - and 'next' on Trump's list
events.description: At the start of this year, Donald Trump ordered the capture and removal of Venezuela's president Nicolas Maduro - he's now facing a trial in New York.

events.title: UK leader Starmer says he is 'furious' he wasn't told Peter Mandelson had failed security checks to be ambassador to US
events.description: UK leader Starmer says he is 'furious' he wasn't told Peter Mandelson had failed security checks to be ambassador to US

events.title: Airline adding bunk beds for economy travelers but bans snacks, smells and cuddling
events.description: Air New Zealand will soon add triple-tier bunk beds for economy travelers to have a chance to sleep on long-haul flights

events.title: Senior British official resigns over Mandelson appointment but Starmer insists he won't go
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has no plans to resign over the appointment of Peter Mandelson as U.K. ambassador to Washington, despite Mandelson failing security checks

events.title: Canadian man facing 14 murder charges to plead guilty to aiding suicide
events.description: The lawyer for a Canadian man facing murder charges for allegedly selling lethal substances online to people at risk of self-harm has told the Canadian Broadcasting Corp. that he has agreed to plead guilty to counseling or aiding suicide

events.title: Gunman kills at least 5 in Kyiv, takes hostages; police shoot him dead
events.description: Gunman kills at least 5 people in Kyiv street and takes hostages; police shoot him dead
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Gunman kills at least 5 in Kyiv, takes hostages; police shoot him dead

events.title: Mexico arrests alleged drug trafficker considered one of Europe's most wanted
events.description: He's wanted in Hungary on drug trafficking accusations, is sought by Interpol.

events.title: Canada Post taking steps to stop home mail delivery
events.description: The government-owned corporation that delivers mail in Canada is taking steps to stop home delivery

events.title: EU officials in Hungary to discuss unlocking billions of euros held while Orbán was in charge
events.description: European Union officials are meeting Friday in Budapest with Hungary's incoming leader Péter Magyar's team

events.title: The Epstein files: Rights experts demand accountability, call for probe into trafficking allegations
events.description: UN independent human rights experts called on Thursday for justice and accountability for young women and girls who were trafficked systematically as part of allegations contained in the so-called Epstein files.

events.title: ‘Lego bandit’ accused in bizarre $34K scheme replacing minifigures with pasta
events.description: Jarrelle Augustine is accused of buying Lego sets from Target, removing valuable pieces, and returning boxes stuffed with pasta, Irvine police say.

events.title: Air Force Academy’s ‘CULEX’ puts thousands of cadets through realistic 24-hour combat simulation
events.description: The CULEX focuses on building confidence, teamwork and leadership skills rather than testing Air Force and Space Force cadets with a pass-or-fail system.

events.title: Video shows teen snatched at bus stop – but victim slips SOS at gas station to escape repeat offender suspect
events.description: A 16-year-old Michigan student was allegedly abducted at gunpoint while waiting for her school bus by a man charged as a three-time habitual offender.

events.title: Trump’s favorite field marshal: Who is Pakistan’s powerful army chief Asim Munir with deep intel ties
events.description: How Pakistan's military chief Asim Munir became one of the few foreign figures trusted by both Trump and Iran's security establishment today.

events.title: Escaped wolf Neukgu returned to South Korean zoo after nine-day search involving thermal imaging drones
events.description: Neukgu, a 2-year-old wolf, escaped a South Korean zoo nine days ago and was safely captured after an extensive search involving drones and more.

events.title: Starmer and Macron accused of ‘playing at being relevant’ with Strait of Hormuz plan
events.description: Britain and France push a European-led naval mission to safeguard shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, separate from U.S. military operations in the region.

events.title: UN filing accuses UK of forced displacement as Diego Garcia tensions and security fears grow
events.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces a crimes against humanity complaint at the U.N. over the alleged forced removal of Chagossian people from the Diego Garcia islands.
